patch.value: Graph planning & structural analytics: critical-path slack, betweenness/closeness centrality, and articulation points/bridges

Add three exact public SDK graph primitives and thin pm graph subcommands filling pm-7ob5 AC bullets 1-3: analyzeRelationshipSchedule (pm graph slack, CPM float over the order-bearing DAG), computeRelationshipCentrality (pm graph centrality, Brandes betweenness + Wasserman-Faust closeness + degree + directed fan-in/out), and findRelationshipCutStructure (pm graph articulation, Tarjan articulation points + bridges as single points of failure).

pm graph slack reports earliest/latest start, total float, critical classification, and makespan over the order-bearing DAG with genuine cycles excluded; pm graph centrality reports exact Brandes betweenness, Wasserman-Faust closeness, undirected degree, and directed fan-in/fan-out ranked deterministically; pm graph articulation reports articulation points and bridges via iterative Tarjan low-link search; all three are exact on the bounded workspace, deterministic, cancellable, carry cost/truncation/cache metadata, honor --kind (centrality/articulation) and --limit/--summary bounds; CLI, MCP, contracts, completion, and docs stay in parity; exact 100/100/100/100 coverage

author: maintainer-agent
message: Implement three planning/structural graph analytics primitives

value.command: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/sdk/graph/scheduling.spec.ts tests/unit/sdk/graph/centrality.spec.ts tests/unit/sdk/graph/run.spec.ts

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check 2026-07-21: all-status search (graph/slack/centrality/betweenness/articulation/critical-path) found no existing item for these three primitives; this is the granular deliverable under umbrella pm-7ob5 (which stays open for structural-holes, temporal what-if, and the million-item eval matrix). Not a duplicate of pm-7ob5 (the 7-bullet umbrella) nor of the shipped communities/redundancy/dominators analytics.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Verification evidence 2026-07-21: full sandboxed coverage passed 324 files / 6038 tests at exact 100/100/100/100 (statements 40605/40605, branches 28585/28585, functions 8240/8240, lines 39504/39504). pnpm lint passed: ESLint 0 warnings, jscpd 0 clones, static-quality (100% docstrings incl. exported+member, complexity), package-boundary, token-budget (14 surfaces). Contracts snapshot regenerated. Manual dogfood in a temp workspace built a diamond+shortcut ordering DAG and confirmed pm graph slack (makespan 4, shortcut task slack 1, critical path A->B->C->D), pm graph centrality (betweenness ranking with directed fan-in/out), and pm graph articulation (correctly reports zero cuts on the biconnected diamond). New unit specs: scheduling.spec.ts (7), centrality.spec.ts (12); integration in run.spec.ts (+3). All three subcommands verified across CLI human + --json + --summary output.

patch.value: Delivered: pm graph slack (analyzeRelationshipSchedule, CPM float), pm graph centrality (computeRelationshipCentrality, Brandes betweenness + Wasserman-Faust closeness + degree + directed fan-in/out), and pm graph articulation (findRelationshipCutStructure, Tarjan articulation points + bridges) as exact deterministic public SDK primitives with full CLI/MCP/contracts/completion/docs parity. Exact 100/100/100/100 coverage over 6038 tests; lint/static/boundary/token gates green; dogfood-verified end to end.

Shipped three exact public SDK graph primitives (analyzeRelationshipSchedule, computeRelationshipCentrality, findRelationshipCutStructure) with pm graph slack/centrality/articulation subcommands and full CLI/MCP/contracts/completion/docs parity.
pm graph exposes CPM slack, betweenness/closeness centrality with fan-in/out, and articulation points/bridges; exact and deterministic; 100% coverage; all gates green.
All three subcommands deliver the specified exact analytics; 6038 tests pass at 100/100/100/100; lint/static/boundary/token gates green; dogfood-verified end to end.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Pre-push Greptile review 207a768a-6eae-4a2f-a76b-81c1931e54d9 (confidence 4/5, safe to merge): accepted both P2 findings. (1) runGraphSlack silently ignored --kind while the cache key still recorded it — fixed by rejecting --kind for slack in assertGraphFlagScope (fail-fast usage error, consistent with --rebuild/--save-baseline scoping) rather than a silent drop; added a rejection regression. (2) GraphScheduleOptions.signal docstring claimed two abort checks but the simplified implementation checks once at entry — corrected the docstring. Greptile confirmed the three algorithms are implemented correctly and well-tested.
